titles: 12 Things Short Girls Look Amazing In Every Time
paragraphs: When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission.Here’s how it works.

titles: Tip #1: Try cropped silhouettes instead of tunic tops
paragraphs: If you, like me, are short, findingtrendsand pieces that work for your height can sometimes be tricky. While actual growth is out of the question, dressing can be made easier simply by knowing what not to wear. If you're wondering why your clothes don't look right, it's entirely possible that you're just wearing the wrong trends.

titles: Tip #2: Try gathered or wrap dresses instead of shirtdresses
paragraphs: From avoiding a particularly tricky print to knowing what silhouettes to stock up on, dressing for your height comes down to a simple set of fashion tips, which is why we put together our edit of the bestclothes for short womenlooking to embrace their stature. Keep reading to find out which fashion trends shorter people should avoid and to shop out top picks for petite girls.

titles: Tip #3: Try tailored layering with suiting separates instead of oversize layering
paragraphs: A cropped style can help elongate any look and is an easy way to add on-trend dimension to your outfit. Pair it with your favorite high-waisted pants for an easy look that won't seem baggy.

titles: Tip #4: Try vertical stripes instead of horizontal
paragraphs: Pair this with your favorite high-waisted skirt, jeans, or shorts.

titles: Tip #5: Try corsets and structured shaping instead of boxy tops
paragraphs: A shirtdress can easily swallow you up and make it look like you're wearing the wrong size or an oversize style if you're petite. Try a gathered or wrap dress instead to ensure your look is falling just right.

titles: Tip #6: Try jumpsuits in a solid color instead of printed ones
paragraphs: A chic, well-tailored pantsuit looks good on any body type, especially on someone petite. A sleek blazer and suit pants are a great dressier option for someone with shorter legs.

titles: Tip #7: Try straight-leg denim instead of loose-fitting jeans
paragraphs: When it comes to blazers, I always vote double breasted.

titles: Tip #8: Try miniskirts instead of midi skirts
paragraphs: Horizontal stripes typically give a widening illusion, while vertical stripes elongate, meaning the latter is usually a go-to for shorter girls.

titles: Tip #9: Try mini bags instead of oversize handbags
paragraphs: Structured tops are a guaranteed way to show off your figure without being swallowed by oversize pieces.

titles: Tip #10: Try a hemline that hits above the knee instead of tea-length dresses
paragraphs: The ultimate going-out top to pair with jeans or cargo pants.

titles: Tip #11: Try ankle boots instead of mid-calf boots
paragraphs: Solid colors are often considered more flattering and give an overall statement look rather than loud prints that can overwhelm a small figure.

titles: Tip #12: Try heels with a low-cut vamp instead of ankle straps
paragraphs: Don't drown yourself in oversize denim. Instead, opt for a straight-leg style that gives the illusion of bagginess while remaining more formfitting.
Midi skirts can cut shorter legs in the wrong place. Opt instead for a miniskirt.
A smaller bag for a smaller person. It's all about proportions.
A shorter hemline lends itself to the look of longer legs. Pairing it with some high heels only adds to that effect.
People will take one look at this dress and compliment you.
Mid-calf or knee-highbootscould cut your legs off in odd places. Try ankle boots instead, so your legs aren't as covered up.
A low-cut vamp on pumps,mules, and flats elongates the legs and is a sleeker option than ankle-strapsandals.
